records.ety: [Of. atteinen, atteignen, atainen, OF. ateindre, ataindre, F. atteindre, fr. L. attingere; ad + tangere to touch, reach. See Tangent, and cf. Attinge, Attaint.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Attained (-tānd"); p. pr. & vb. n. Attaining.]
usage: Attain always implies an effort toward an object. Hence it is not synonymous with obtain and procure, which do not necessarily imply such effort or motion. We procure or obtain a thing by purchase or loan, and we obtain by inheritance, but we do not attain it by such means.

defs: To achieve or accomplish, that is, to reach by efforts; to gain; to compass; as, to attain rest.
quotes: Is he wise who hopes to attain the end without the means?

defs: To gain or obtain possession of; to acquire.
To get at the knowledge of; to ascertain.
To reach or come to, by progression or motion; to arrive at.
To reach in excellence or degree; to equal.
To come or arrive, by motion, growth, bodily exertion, or efforts toward a place, object, state, etc.; to reach.
quotes: If by any means they might attain to Phenice.
Nor nearer might the dogs attain.
To see your trees attain to the dignity of timber.
Few boroughs had as yet attained to power such as this.

defs: To come or arrive, by an effort of mind.
quotes: Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high, I can not attain unto it.
